competitor_patterns: Extreme long-form depth: Bikerumor's top-ranking 'Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026' post has 24,903 words — nearly 6x our best post (4,045 words). Top competitors treat these as definitive guides, not articles.

Named product H2s as ranking anchors: Bikerumor uses individual H2s per product (Julbo Density, Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour) — this captures brand + model long-tail queries and signals comprehensive coverage to Google.

Rich structured content mix: Top competitors combine FAQs, comparison tables, expert commentary sections, and user reviews in a single page — our 'Best Road Cycling Glasses' post has a table and FAQ but lacks real review content and expert voice.

Domain authority used as editorial trust signal: Bikerumor leads with 'Bikerumor Tested' in title — emphasising hands-on testing credentials, not just curation. Our titles lack a trust/methodology signal.

Interchangeable lens keyword gap is wide open: Alibaba holds position 1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with a 404 error page — a high-value keyword with zero quality competition that we are not targeting.
our_gaps: Word count is 4-6x below top competitors: Our best post is 4,045 words vs Bikerumor's 24,903. We are unlikely to outrank established domains without matching content depth on high-impression keywords like 'best cycling glasses 2026' (220 impressions, pos 8.4).

No individual competitor product deep-dives as H2s: We use thematic H2s (Criteria, Comparison, Scenarios) rather than named-product H2s, missing all the long-tail traffic from queries like 'Oakley Sutro Lite review' or 'POC Devour review 2026'.

Missing a dedicated 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' page: Our core product differentiator (VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione click-in lenses) has no dedicated landing page or post, despite Alibaba ranking #1 with a 404 — this is our single biggest untapped keyword.

No real-world test narrative or ride-log content: Competitor snippets reference specific terrain conditions ('smooth surfaces', 'wider heads', budget comparisons from real rides). Our posts describe features but lack first-person ride-tested authority signals.

'Oakley Alternatives' post (pos 6.8, 219 impressions) lacks a comparison table: The post has no table, which is a structural disadvantage vs competitors who use tables to win featured snippets and rank above us.
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses — Alibaba ranks #1 with a 404 error; zero quality content exists; directly matches our core StradaPro feature; high purchase intent.
lightweight cycling sunglasses 2026 — our 25g frame is a measurable differentiator; no existing Velluto content targets this angle; buyers searching this are comparison-shopping on a specific spec.
anti-fog cycling glasses — built-in feature we own but have never made a content focus; informational and commercial intent; easy to rank for with a dedicated post.

cycling glasses 30-day trial / try before you buy cycling sunglasses — unique commercial offer no competitor replicates; captures high-intent buyers who are risk-averse; zero competition on this angle.

POC Devour alternative / Julbo Density alternative — Bikerumor's top-ranking post names these products in H2s; we can capture comparative traffic from riders researching these specific models against alternatives.
next_post_guidelines: Target minimum 6,000 words for any post competing on 'best cycling glasses/sunglasses 2026' keywords — match Bikerumor's depth tier; use named competitor product H2s (Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour, Julbo Density, Tifosi Rail) to capture long-tail brand+model queries.

Add a dedicated H2 section per competitor product reviewed, structured as: [Product Name]: Pros, Cons, Who It's For, How It Compares to Velluto StradaPro — this captures named-product searches and builds internal comparison logic.

Write the 'Best Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses 2026' post immediately — target this keyword with a full guide leading with VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione system; include a comparison table of all click-in lens systems on the market; this is a near-zero-competition opportunity.

Every post must include a methodology/testing section in the intro (e.g. 'Tested over 400km across road and gravel') — this matches Bikerumor's 'Tested' trust signal and improves E-E-A-T signals for AI overviews and Google ranking.

Add a comparison table to the 'Best Oakley Alternatives' post immediately — it has 219 impressions at pos 6.8 and no table; a table with columns (Weight, Lens System, Price, Trial Policy, UV Rating) could push it to top 3 and win a featured snippet.

Incorporate specific measurable claims in every H1 and intro paragraph: '25g', '30-day trial', 'UV400', 'tool-free lens swap in 8 seconds' — these are concrete differentiators that resonate in AI-generated summaries and comparison snippets.

Build an FAQ section with at minimum 8 questions per post targeting voice search and AI overview patterns — questions like 'What is the lightest cycling glasses in 2026?', 'Do Velluto lenses fog up?', 'Can I try cycling glasses before buying?' directly feed AI answer engines.
seo_quick_wins: Add a named-product comparison table to every 'best of' and 'alternatives' post — include columns for Weight (g), Lens Interchangeability, Price (€), Trial Policy, Anti-Fog — this targets featured snippets and addresses the missing table gap in the Oakley Alternatives post.

Update all meta titles to include the year '2026' and a measurable hook (lightest, tested, trial) — our current titles are descriptive but not click-driving; year + superlative + trust signal is the pattern that wins CTR at positions 5-9.

Add internal links from every existing post to a future 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses' pillar page — build topical authority around this core product differentiator before competitors fill the Alibaba-404 gap.
meta_title_fixes: QUERY: 'veluto' (239 impressions, 0.4% CTR, pos 5.5) → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses — Official Store | velluto-shop.com | Why: The query is a misspelling of our brand; the current result likely shows a generic page. A clear brand + official store signal reassures the searcher they've found the right brand, lifting CTR from navigational intent. Ensure the page also includes 'Did you mean Velluto?' on-page to capture misspelling traffic.

QUERY: 'oakley sunglasses alternative' (67 impressions, 1.5% CTR, pos 6.7) → New title: Best Oakley Sunglasses Alternative 2026 — 25g, 30-Day Trial | Why: Adds year for freshness, leads with our two strongest differentiators (ultralight weight + risk-free trial) that Oakley cannot match, directly answers the search intent.

QUERY: 'oakley radar alternative' (48 impressions, 2.1% CTR, pos 4.1) → New title: Oakley Radar Alternative 2026: Lighter, Cheaper, Tested | Why: At pos 4.1 we are very close to top 3; a title that names the specific model being replaced, adds 'Lighter' (25g claim), 'Cheaper' (value angle), and 'Tested' (trust signal) will sharply improve CTR and could push to pos 1-3.

QUERY: 'oakley alternative' (46 impressions, 2.2% CTR, pos 6.5) → New title: Oakley Alternative for Cyclists 2026 — Try Free for 30 Days | Why: Adds cycling specificity (filters non-cycling traffic), year signal, and the 30-day trial CTA which is a unique conversion hook no Oakley competitor can match.
content_quick_wins: QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (pos 8.4, 220 impressions) → Topic: Expand 'Best Road Cycling Glasses 2026' post to 8,000+ words with named-product H2s for each competitor (Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour, Julbo Density, Tifosi Rail, Rudy Project) | Add: Individual H2 per product with pros/cons/who-it's-for table, a weight comparison chart leading with Velluto's 25g, and a 'How We Tested' methodology section — this directly matches Bikerumor's structure that holds pos 1.

QUERY: 'oakley alternatives' (pos 6.8, 219 impressions) → Topic: Rebuild the 'Best Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026' post with a full comparison table and 5,000+ words | Add: A scannable comparison table (missing from current post) with Weight, Price, Lens System, Trial Policy columns; add named H2 for each alternative brand; add a 'Velluto vs Oakley Sutro Lite' head-to-head section as the anchor — tables are the primary missing element vs top competitors.

QUERY: 'best cycling glasses 2026' (pos 7.3, 132 impressions) → Topic: Create a standalone 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: Complete Ranked List' post distinct from the road-specific post, covering road, gravel, and commuting use cases | Add: A ranked list with position numbers (#1, #2… #8), a 'Best for Each Type of Rider' summary table, and specific ride-condition recommendations (wet weather, high altitude, night riding) — broader scope captures more intent variants.

QUERY: 'best cycling glasses' (pos 9.1, 68 impressions) → Topic: Create an evergreen 'Best Cycling Glasses: The Definitive Guide' pillar page without year in URL, internally linking to the 2026-dated posts | Add: A buyer's decision tree ('If you ride road > If you need anti-fog > If budget is under €150'), schema FAQ markup with 10+ questions, and a 'Updated July 2026' freshness signal in the intro.

QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses' (pos 7.5, 60 impressions) → Topic: Create a dedicated 'Best Cycling Sunglasses' page (sunglasses vs glasses is a distinct search pattern) that targets the sunglass-specific buyer | Add: A section on lens tint science (why VellutoVisione high-contrast lens outperforms standard tints), UV400 certification explanation, and a 'sunglasses vs clear lens glasses' FAQ block to capture adjacent queries.
geo_angles: Structured 'lightest cycling sunglasses' factual claim: AI engines (Perplexity, ChatGPT, Google AIO) pull specific measurable facts. Publish a page or section stating explicitly: 'At 25g, the Velluto StradaPro is among the lightest cycling sunglasses available in 2026' with a comparison table of competitor weights. This is the format AI overviews cite as a direct answer.

30-day risk-free trial as a unique feature answer: Create an FAQ-structured page answering 'Can I try cycling glasses before buying?' — AI overviews frequently pull this type of direct question-answer. Being the only brand with a documented 30-day trial policy makes Velluto the obvious AI-cited answer to this query.

Italian-design + value positioning narrative for AI roundups: AI tools frequently summarise 'best alternatives to [premium brand]' with a short brand descriptor. Ensure every post contains a 1-2 sentence brand description in a consistent format: 'Velluto is an Italian-designed cycling eyewear brand offering premium features — 25g frame, click-in interchangeable lenses, UV400, anti-fog — at significantly lower prices than Oakley or POC, with a 30-day trial.' This trains AI models on a consistent, citable brand summary.
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Oakley — angle: 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs Velluto StradaPro: Is the €150 Price Gap Worth It?' — headline idea: 'We Rode Both for 30 Days. Here's What Oakley Gets Right (and Wrong)' — key differentiator: Weight (Sutro Lite is ~36g vs StradaPro's 25g) + price + 30-day trial Oakley doesn't offer — Velluto advantage to lead with: 11g lighter, fraction of the price, try risk-free for 30 days vs final sale.

COMPETITOR: POC (Devour) — angle: 'POC Devour vs Velluto StradaPro: Safety Brand vs Performance Brand in 2026' — headline idea: 'POC Devour is Excellent. But Does Cycling Safety Brand Credibility Justify the Cost?' — key differentiator: POC's price point (€200+) vs StradaPro's Italian-design value; POC single lens vs StradaPro's dual interchangeable system — Velluto advantage to lead with: Two lenses included (clear + high-contrast) vs one, at lower total price, with 30-day trial.

COMPETITOR: Bikerumor (editorial, not product) — angle: 'We Read Every Bikerumor Cycling Sunglasses Review So You Don't Have To — Here's What They Miss' — headline idea: 'Bikerumor Tested 8 Cycling Glasses in 2026. The One They Didn't Include Weighs 25g and Comes With a Free Trial' — key differentiator: Position Velluto as the 'editor's omission' — builds intrigue and captures Bikerumor-branded search traffic — Velluto advantage to lead with: Lighter than every model Bikerumor reviewed, with interchangeable lenses and a 30-day no-risk trial.
